title: AI Content Prioritization - Geographic Relevance
content_md:
# AI Content Prioritization - Geographic Relevance

- Code: 82487a41
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=82487a41afb1469786ee7940c70e9558
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/071dd9b80f524bbfbee6224fe95309a0?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 2m 5s
- Resources:
  - [Leadsnap](https://app.leadsnap.com/register-caleb-ulku)

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: All right, so let's talk about how
to prioritize geographical content production.
If your rank map looks like this,
then you don't need geographical content production.
What you need is just to finish up the Core 30
and then topical relevance if it continues to look like that.
So instead, I ran this example of a business
that's doing pretty well, but we want to prioritize content
to turn even more of these green.
So when we're prioritizing content,
we're looking for fours and fives,
and we're going to write geographical content
about those fours and fives.
But you can see there are quite a few fours and fives here.
So which ones do we want to prioritize?
So that's what this prompt is going to help with.
So we're going to hit Share, and then we're
going to export that CSV.
I'm going to come over to Claude,
and you can see I've copied the exact prompt that's
just below this video.
And we'll come over here, and we'll give it the heat map,
and then I'm going to change the model
to make sure we're using Opus.
Opus is the better model for doing data analysis.
And then we'll go ahead and run it.
All right, that took a little bit of time, a minute or two,
but it is finished now.
And what this AI, what the prompt does
is it gets the AI to analyze the rank map based
on the longitude and latitude and sorts them
in terms of how hard they are to rank
and what the traffic potential is.
So it's basically giving a high priority
to the West Central Fort Wayne.
It gives you why it's giving that one.
It's giving some target keywords.
It's doing this based on demographics,
based on population density, based on all the information
that would make this really hard to do manually.
So essentially, and if we scroll all the way down,
it's giving those neighborhoods the topic content
recommendations.
This is a good prompt to just very quickly get
an idea of what type of content focused
on which geographical locations we should be focused on.
